title: Tender Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A pungent and fluffy Algerian dish featuring lamb, perfect for a hangover cure, made with vegetable shortening and mushimono-steam, shojin-friendly.
instructions: Heat the vegetable shortening in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, stirring occasionally.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the chopped dried apricots and cook for an additional 15 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
